task: I’m revisiting a project template after a recent edit cycle exposed a few weak spots, and I want to tighten it up before the next round starts. Can you help me think through a cleaner way to set it up so the handoff and revisions are easier to manage?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — a cleaner template can make handoffs and revision cycles much smoother.

A good way to tighten it up is to structure the template around **clarity, ownership, and change tracking**. Here’s a simple framework you can adapt:

### 1) Start with a short project summary
Include:
- Project name
- Goal / outcome
- Audience or client
- Current phase
- Deadline or key milestones

This gives anyone picking it up immediate context.

### 2) Separate “stable” info from “editable” info
Use clear sections like:
- **Overview**
- **Requirements**
- **Deliverables**
- **Open questions**
- **Revision history**
- **Next actions**

That makes it easier to see what’s fixed versus what’s still in motion.

### 3) Add explicit ownership
For each task or section, note:
- Owner
- Reviewer / approver
- Due date
- Status

This reduces confusion during handoff.

### 4) Build in a revision log
A simple table helps a lot:
- Date
- Change made
- Who requested it
- Who approved it
- Impact / notes

This becomes especially useful after multiple edit cycles.

### 5) Use a “handoff checklist”
Before passing it on, confirm:
- All required files are included
- Links are working
- Final version is labeled
- Outstanding questions are listed
- Next person knows what to do first

### 6) Make versioning obvious
Use a consistent naming pattern like:
- `ProjectName_v1`
- `ProjectName_v1.1`
- `ProjectName_FINAL`
  
Or, better:
- `ProjectName_2026-04-29`
- `ProjectName_2026-04-29_revA`

### 7) Keep revision comments separate from the main content
If feedback gets mixed into the body of the template, it becomes harder to clean up later. A dedicated feedback section or comment log keeps things tidy.

If you want, I can help you turn this into:
1. a **one-page reusable template**,  
2. a **handoff-focused checklist**, or  
3. a **revision-tracking format** you can drop into your current workflow.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is in the right lane, but it’s still pretty generic. I’d want it shaped more around actual post workflow — source links, project path, audio notes, graphics status, export settings, naming/version rules, who signed off what, that kind of stuff.

The weak spot for me is usually not the idea of a template, it’s where the handoff breaks. So I’d tighten it around:
- intake / source media
- current cut status
- open issues
- revision requests
- delivery specs
- archive / backup location

If you can, give me a version that’s more like a practical edit-room template, not a general project template. Maybe with a clean section order I could actually use in a shared doc or project notes file.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — for an edit-room workflow, I’d make the template function like a **handoff control sheet** rather than a broad project brief.

Here’s a practical section order you could use in a shared doc, project notes file, or production tracker.

---

# Edit Room Handoff Template

## 1) Project Header
- **Project name:**
- **Client / show / channel:**
- **Editor:**
- **Producer / approver:**
- **Date started:**
- **Current version:**
- **Status:** `Intake / Rough cut / Fine cut / Client review / Final / Archived`

---

## 2) Source Media / Intake
Use this to make sure nothing gets lost at the start.

- **Project path:**
- **Source location:**
- **Shared drive / cloud link:**
- **Raw media status:** `Complete / Partial / Missing`
- **Audio source notes:**
- **Graphics / logo / brand assets:**
- **Music / SFX / licensed assets:**
- **Reference links:**
- **Transcripts / scripts / notes:**

Optional table:

| Asset | Location | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---:|---|
| Camera A | link/path | Complete | synced |
| Audio WAVs | link/path | Missing | waiting on upload |
| Graphics | link/path | Complete | approved |

---

## 3) Current Cut Status
This is the “where are we right now” section.

- **Timeline / sequence name:**
- **Current cut length:**
- **What’s already locked:**
- **What’s still in progress:**
- **Known technical issues:**
- **Graphics status:** `Not started / In progress / Awaiting approval / Locked`
- **Audio status:** `Temp / Mixed / Needs cleanup / Final`
- **Captions / subtitles status:**
- **Color status:**
- **Export status:**

---

## 4) Open Issues
Keep this short and visible so the next person knows what can block delivery.

| Issue | Priority | Owner | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Missing b-roll | High | Producer | Open | waiting on upload |
| Audio pop at 01:12 | Medium | Editor | In progress | fix in next pass |

You can also list:
- **Blocked by:**
- **Needs approval from:**
- **Questions outstanding:**

---

## 5) Revision Requests
This is where handoffs often break, so I’d make it explicit.

| Rev # | Requested by | Date | Request | Applied? | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Client | 04/29 | shorten intro | Yes | cut from 12s to 6s |
| 2 | Producer | 04/29 | swap lower third | No | waiting on new graphic |

If you want it even cleaner, separate:
- **Requested changes**
- **Implemented changes**
- **Rejected / deferred changes**

---

## 6) Delivery Specs
This should be unambiguous and easy to check before export.

- **Master format:**
- **Resolution:**
- **Frame rate:**
- **Aspect ratio:**
- **Audio spec:**
- **Codec / container:**
- **Color space / gamma:**
- **Subtitles / captions required:** `Yes / No`
- **Thumbnail / still required:** `Yes / No`
- **Platform-specific settings:**
- **Export preset name:**

Example:
- 1920x1080
- 23.976 fps
- H.264 .mp4
- Stereo audio, -14 LUFS target
- Rec.709

---

## 7) Naming / Version Rules
This is one of the biggest handoff protection points.

### File naming format
Use one consistent pattern, such as:
`ProjectName_CutType_Version_Date_Initials`

Example:
`BrandLaunch_RoughCut_v03_2026-04-29_AB`

### Version rules
- **v01** = first assembled cut
- **v02+** = revision passes
- **vFinal** only when fully approved
- Never overwrite exported finals
- Keep dated exports for major review rounds

### Sequence / export naming
- **Edit sequence:**
- **Review export:**
- **Final export:**
- **Archive export:**

---

## 8) Sign-Off Log
This makes approval traceable.

| Item | Approved by | Date | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Rough cut | Producer | 04/29 | proceed to fine cut |
| Graphics | Client | 04/29 | approved as-is |
| Final audio | Audio lead | 04/29 | approved |

You can also include:
- **Pending sign-off**
- **Conditional approval**
- **Approved with notes**

---

## 9) Export / Delivery Checklist
A quick final pass section.

- [ ] Correct sequence exported
- [ ] Correct version number used
- [ ] Audio checked
- [ ] Graphics visible and correct
- [ ] Captions included if required
- [ ] File plays back fully
- [ ] Delivery spec confirmed
- [ ] Review link uploaded
- [ ] Client/producer notified

---

## 10) Archive / Backup
This is where the handoff becomes recoverable later.

- **Archive location:**
- **Backup location:**
- **Project folder path:**
- **Final export path:**
- **Working files path:**
- **Notes on what was archived:**
- **Anything excluded from archive:**

Suggested archive structure:
- `/ProjectName/01_Source`
- `/ProjectName/02_ProjectFiles`
- `/ProjectName/03_Exports`
- `/ProjectName/04_Review`
- `/ProjectName/05_Archive`

---

# Clean Section Order
If you want the shortest usable order for a shared notes doc, I’d use:

1. **Project Header**  
2. **Source Media / Intake**  
3. **Current Cut Status**  
4. **Open Issues**  
5. **Revision Requests**  
6. **Delivery Specs**  
7. **Naming / Version Rules**  
8. **Sign-Off Log**  
9. **Export / Delivery Checklist**  
10. **Archive / Backup**

---

# A simple rule to make it work
If a handoff is failing, it’s usually because one of these is unclear:
- where the source lives
- what version is current
- what changed in the last round
- what still needs approval
- what exact export is required
- where the final files are stored

So the template should force those answers to be visible at a glance.

If you want, I can turn this into a **copy-paste doc template** with blank fields, or a **more compact one-page version** for daily edit notes.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, closer. Still a little too ‘template-y’ though. I’d want the order to match how the handoff actually moves: intake, current state, what changed, what’s blocked, what gets exported, where it lives after.

The main thing for me is making the failure points impossible to miss. So I’d probably want sections like:
- source / intake
- project path + working sequence
- current cut status
- revision notes by round
- audio / gfx / captions status
- delivery specs
- sign-off / approvals
- export + archive locations

Also, I’d want a spot for **notes that are only for the next person** — like if I’m handing off to another editor, or to online / audio / producer. That’s usually where things get muddy.

If you rewrite it once more, make it feel like something I could paste straight into a shared doc and actually use without cleaning it up first.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a more **handoff-first, edit-room style** version you can paste straight into a shared doc.

---

# EDIT HANDOFF NOTES

## 1) Source / Intake
**Project:**  
**Client / Show:**  
**Date received:**  
**Requested by:**  
**Current owner:**  

**Source media location:**  
**Project path:**  
**Shared drive / folder link:**  
**Reference links:**  
**Notes on intake:**  

**Source status**
- [ ] All media received
- [ ] Audio received
- [ ] Graphics received
- [ ] Captions / transcript received
- [ ] Missing items noted

---

## 2) Working Sequence / Current File
**Current working sequence:**  
**Sequence path:**  
**Current version:**  
**Last updated:**  
**Working notes:**  

**What this file is for:**  
**What not to touch / avoid overwriting:**  

---

## 3) Current Cut Status
**Stage:** `intake / rough cut / fine cut / picture lock / online / final`

**Current state:**  
**What’s already done:**  
**What still needs work:**  
**Known problems:**  

**Locked / approved elements**
- [ ] Structure
- [ ] Timing
- [ ] Audio
- [ ] Graphics
- [ ] Captions
- [ ] Color

---

## 4) Revision Notes by Round
### Round 1
**Requested by:**  
**Date:**  
**Notes / changes requested:**  
**Changes made:**  
**Still open:**  

### Round 2
**Requested by:**  
**Date:**  
**Notes / changes requested:**  
**Changes made:**  
**Still open:**  

### Round 3
**Requested by:**  
**Date:**  
**Notes / changes requested:**  
**Changes made:**  
**Still open:**  

---

## 5) Audio / Graphics / Captions Status
### Audio
**Status:** `temp / clean-up needed / mixed / final`  
**Notes:**  

### Graphics
**Status:** `missing / in progress / awaiting approval / locked`  
**Notes:**  

### Captions / Subtitles
**Status:** `not started / in progress / delivered / approved`  
**Notes:**  

---

## 6) Blockers / Open Issues
**What is blocking progress:**  
**Who needs to respond:**  
**Priority:**  
**Deadline / due date:**  

| Issue | Owner | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
|  |  |  |  |

---

## 7) Delivery Specs
**Required format:**  
**Resolution:**  
**Frame rate:**  
**Aspect ratio:**  
**Codec / container:**  
**Audio spec:**  
**Color space:**  
**Caption requirement:**  
**Thumbnail / stills required:**  
**Platform-specific notes:**  

**Export preset to use:**  

---

## 8) Sign-Off / Approvals
| Item | Approved by | Date | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Picture |  |  |  |
| Audio |  |  |  |
| Graphics |  |  |  |
| Captions |  |  |  |
| Final export |  |  |  |

**Pending approvals:**  
**Conditional approvals:**  

---

## 9) Export / Archive
**Final export location:**  
**Review export location:**  
**Archive folder:**  
**Backup location:**  
**Final filename:**  
**Working file archive notes:**  

**Export checklist**
- [ ] Correct version
- [ ] Correct spec
- [ ] Audio checked
- [ ] Graphics checked
- [ ] Captions included if needed
- [ ] File uploaded to correct location
- [ ] Handoff complete

---

## 10) Notes for the Next Person
**This section is only for the next editor / online / audio / producer:**  

-  
-  
-  

**Things to know before touching this project:**  
**Things that will break if changed:**  
**Best next action:**  

---

If you want, I can also turn this into:
1. a **more compact one-page version**, or  
2. a **cleaner production-style version with fewer headings and more fill-in fields**.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is usable. The order makes sense now — feels like an actual handoff sheet, not a generic project brief.

The “notes for the next person” section is the piece I was missing. That’s usually where the real damage happens if it’s not explicit.

I’d probably use this as-is and trim a couple labels to match whatever shop I’m in, but the structure is right. Good enough to paste into a shared doc and start using without a lot of cleanup.
